verified_current_state.claim: Current RuntimeService is constructed in the GUI/QML bridge process.
Current UiGrpcBridge wraps RuntimeService for QML and is not evidence of real gRPC transport to a separate Core process.
Application bootstrap uses PySide QGuiApplication path; M0.3 does not change it.
Generated protobuf includes RuntimeService gRPC stubs and proto declares RuntimeService; this is source material for logical IPC, not proof of separate CoreHost process.
Desktop shell plan and phase foundation mention IPC/gRPC as architecture source material, not complete tray/background/autostart implementation evidence.
Windows packaging/smoke tests exist and are evidence only for packaging/smoke coverage, not process separation.

process_roles.name: core_host
process_roles.purpose: Authoritative runtime host for trading state, exchange adapters, market data, strategy execution, risk, persistence, ledger, reconciliation, execution leases, audit and client read models.
process_roles.lifecycle_owner: desktop_user_session supervisor initially; future windows_service_manager in service deployment; never DesktopShell
process_roles.multiplicity: one active CoreHost per local state store and DeviceInstallation
responsibilities: single source of truth for trading runtime

process_roles.name: tray_agent
process_roles.purpose: Windows notification-area client and desktop-session supervisor for status, notifications, DesktopShell restore, optional Compact Status HUD and reconnect.
process_roles.multiplicity: one active TrayAgent per logged-in user session
responsibilities: reconnect after GUI or IPC failure
forbidden_responsibilities: place orders except by authorized Core command

process_roles.name: desktop_shell
process_roles.purpose: Full PySide/QML client for dashboard, terminal, configuration, wizard, charts, read-model presentation and authorized Core commands.
process_roles.lifecycle_owner: interactive user request and DesktopShell process
process_roles.multiplicity: initial release may enforce one main GUI process per user session; Core protocol supports multiple read-only clients

process_roles.name: bootstrapper
process_roles.purpose: One-shot launcher and discovery helper for CoreHost, TrayAgent, DesktopShell-on-demand and future maintenance/update mode.
responsibilities: consume protected MaintenanceAuthorization handoff for update/restart workflow

future_external_roles.name: windows_service_manager
future_external_roles.purpose: May supervise CoreHost in future service deployment mode

deployment_modes.name: desktop_user_session
guarantees: CoreHost runs under Windows user account
TrayAgent runs in logged-in user session
Core continues after Windows lock screen
non_guarantees: does not promise operation after user logoff
does not promise operation while system is powered off

deployment_modes.name: windows_service
guarantees: CoreHost may start with the system
CoreHost may run without logged-in user session
TrayAgent and DesktopShell attach after logon
Protobuf/IPC and domain model stay compatible
deployment_modes.non_equivalence: Must not be treated automatically as equivalent to desktop_user_session

authority_boundaries: CoreHost is the only authority for mutable trading state
GUI state cannot be fresher than Core-confirmed state
GUI must not show command success before Core acknowledgement
GUI and Tray do not communicate directly with exchanges
GUI and Tray do not edit state files as Core control plane
Shared files are not a control plane
All mutating commands use versioned IPC
Mutating commands include command_id, correlation_id, client_instance_id, operator authorization context when required, expected protocol/schema version, accepted/rejected result and audit reference
GUI lock and operator authentication differ from execution authorization
Tray/HUD are read-only except explicitly defined operator commands

heartbeat_and_reconnect: clients detect Core loss and enter DISCONNECTED/UNREACHABLE
stale data is visibly marked stale
after reconnect full snapshot is fetched before streams
unacknowledged mutating command outcome is unknown and reconciled by command_id
non-idempotent command is not replayed without status check

discovery_contract.acl_required: descriptor file and IPC endpoint limited to correct user/installation
discovery_contract.transport_secret_source: secure store or protected OS mechanism if needed

core_host: one active CoreHost per local state store / DeviceInstallation
local process lock blocks second Core on same data
process lock does not replace ExchangeAccount ExecutionLease
ExecutionLease protects account across devices/runtime instances
tray_agent: one active instance per logged-in user session
second instance forwards show/open to existing instance and exits
desktop_shell: initial release may enforce one main GUI process per session
Core protocol supports multiple read-only clients
future web/mobile panel must not require base Core contract change

startup_sequence.description: Resolve DeviceInstallation and canonical local state-store identity before any mutable initialization.
Atomically acquire the local process lock for that DeviceInstallation/state-store identity.
If the lock is busy, do not open mutable state store, do not create RuntimeSession, do not initialize adapters, and return information about the existing Core.
After owning the lock, create the RuntimeSession.
Open the mutable state store only after the process lock is held.
Read durable kill switch state and last trading environment.
Load configuration and secure-store references without putting secrets in command line.
Connect accounts as allowed by readiness gates, perform reconciliation, and acquire required ExecutionLease records.
Enter READY only after startup readiness, reconciliation, authorization, lease, kill-switch and capability/policy gates pass.
DesktopShell opens only by user preference, setup need, or request.

active_core_or_strategies.title: CryptoHunter nadal pracuje
rules: stop_core_with_secondary_confirmation opens controlled shutdown dialog and does not stop immediately
cancel returns to app
click X is not kill switch
click X does not change active trading environment
click X does not reset operator authentication or ExecutionLease
background hides or exits DesktopShell while TrayAgent remains active
HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND does not change Core lifecycle
Core may be running, unreachable, stopping, or in supervised restart
UI must show the actual Core state

shutdown_intents.name: CLOSE_DESKTOP_SHELL
invariants: stops only DesktopShell
does not stop Core
does not stop TrayAgent
does not stop strategies

conditions: silent ordinary close allowed only when Core process health is NOT_STARTED or STOPPED
silent ordinary close requires core supervision restart state NONE
silent ordinary close requires no active runtime
silent ordinary close requires sufficiently confirmed Core state
close must not leave an active Core without TrayAgent
Core IPC reachability is UNREACHABLE
Core process health is CRASHED
core supervision restart state is SCHEDULED or IN_PROGRESS
observed Core state may be stale
closing may leave a running or restarting Core without visible GUI
message_requirements: Core may still be running or may be automatically started
closing the window does not stop Core

state_cases.reason: Confirmed inactive Core may close silently.
Crashed unscheduled Core can be closed only after warning.
forbidden: scheduled restart cannot be closed without a confirmed running TrayAgent; GUI remains visible or opens controlled shutdown/cancel path.
route_to_background_policy: supervised restart with Tray available must use HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND or controlled shutdown/cancel path.
route_to_background_policy: reachable supervised restart with Tray available must use HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND or controlled shutdown/cancel path.
forbidden: supervised restart cannot be closed without a confirmed running TrayAgent; GUI remains visible or opens controlled shutdown/cancel path.
Restart exhausted needs visible operator action; warning required.
use HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND or controlled shutdown dialog
Pending scheduled restart with stale cached observation and no running Tray is forbidden; GUI stays visible or offers controlled shutdown/cancel.
Pending scheduled restart with no current observation and no running Tray is forbidden; GUI stays visible or offers controlled shutdown/cancel.
Restart in progress with stale cached observation and no running Tray is forbidden; GUI stays visible or offers controlled shutdown/cancel.
Unreachable non-restart Core with stale cached observation; close warns/acknowledges and never treats cache as current.
Unreachable non-restart Core with no observation; close warns/acknowledges and fails closed on current-state claims.
Scheduled restart confirmed by independent proof with running Tray; close is forbidden and routes to hide/background or controlled shutdown/cancel.
Scheduled restart confirmed by Tray supervisor; close is forbidden and routes to hide/background or controlled shutdown/cancel.
Tray supervisor confirms crashed Core; close may proceed with acknowledgement and must not claim Core is running.
Tray supervisor confirms restart exhaustion; close may proceed with acknowledgement and message requires operator action.

shutdown_intents.name: HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND
shutdown_intents.description: Hide or exit DesktopShell while TrayAgent remains active and the current Core lifecycle or supervised-restart lifecycle continues unchanged.
invariants: requires active TrayAgent
strategies remain governed by Core policy
only background may be remembered
HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND does not change Core process health
HIDE_TO_BACKGROUND does not change supervision restart state
TrayAgent remains active and supervises Core or its restart
strategies remain controlled only by actual Core state

conditions: TrayAgent process health must be HEALTHY or DEGRADED
TrayAgent must actually be running
Core IPC reachability must be REACHABLE or an explicit transitional-disconnect policy must be shown
no silent hide when both Core and Tray are unreachable
CRASHED is allowed only with a running TrayAgent
SCHEDULED or IN_PROGRESS means Tray supervises restart
EXHAUSTED is shown as operator-action-required state
must not display Core running when Core process health is CRASHED
only this intent may be remembered as default for active Core
Core IPC reachability is UNREACHABLE
Core process health is CRASHED
core supervision restart state is SCHEDULED or IN_PROGRESS
Core state is uncertain
message_requirements: Core may be unreachable, restarting, or still working despite IPC loss
TrayAgent remains responsible for visible status and supervision
must not claim Core is running when Core process health is CRASHED
user can cancel instead of hiding to background

state_cases.reason: Active reachable Core can continue under Tray visibility.
STARTING + IN_PROGRESS is supervised restart; Tray remains active and acknowledgement is required. IPC unreachable; Tray supervisor confirms supervised restart.
STARTING + IN_PROGRESS is supervised restart; Tray remains active and acknowledgement is required. IPC reachable through Core handshake during supervised restart.
Scheduled restart with stale cached observation may hide only after acknowledgement while Tray remains running; Core lifecycle and supervision restart state are unchanged.
Pending restart with stale observation and no running Tray cannot hide the GUI.
Pending restart with unknown observation and no running Tray cannot hide the GUI.
Hide with running Tray and stale cached non-restart Core observation requires acknowledgement; Core lifecycle is unchanged.
Hide with running Tray and no Core observation requires acknowledgement; Core lifecycle is unchanged.
Unscheduled crash confirmed by independent proof may hide only while Tray remains available and acknowledgement is shown.
Unscheduled crash confirmed by Tray supervisor may hide only while Tray remains available and acknowledgement is shown.
Scheduled restart confirmed by independent proof may hide only with running Tray; supervision state is unchanged.
Scheduled restart confirmed by Tray supervisor may hide only with running Tray; supervision state is unchanged.
OPERATOR_ACTION_REQUIRED restart exhaustion confirmed by independent proof may hide only with running Tray.
OPERATOR_ACTION_REQUIRED restart exhaustion confirmed by Tray supervisor may hide only with running Tray.
Forbidden: active reachable Core cannot be hidden when Tray is unavailable.
Forbidden: independently confirmed Core state cannot be hidden when Tray is unavailable.
Forbidden: stale cached Core state cannot be hidden when Tray is unavailable.
Forbidden: absent Core observation cannot be hidden when Tray is unavailable.

startup_readiness_states.name: SETUP_REQUIRED

no private exchange connections
no exchange-secret loading
no strategies

rules: runtime may proceed according to policy

startup_readiness_states.name: BLOCKED
rules: diagnostics may remain available

start_core_on_logon.description: Start Core on Windows logon only after setup wizard completion.

open_desktop_shell_on_logon.description: Do not open full DesktopShell on logon by default.

show_hud_on_logon.description: Follow user preference; default is false and HUD requires TrayAgent.

resume_runtime_after_reconciliation.description: Separate explicit preference; not implied by Core autostart.

resume requires reconciliation, lease, capability, policy and non-triggered kill switch

before wizard completion autostart does not start trading

valid_core_lifecycle_pairs.description: Core process is not started and has no supervised restart pending.
Normal user/session startup is in progress.
Supervisor is actively starting Core after a crash.
Core is healthy with no restart pending.
Core is degraded but running with no restart pending.
Core is stopping under normal lifecycle.
Core is stopped with no restart pending.
Core has crashed and no restart has been scheduled yet.
Core has crashed and restart is scheduled after backoff.
Core has crashed and restart attempts are exhausted; operator action is required.

state_predicates.description: Independent process-lock/descriptor proof confirms no active runtime; cached STOPPED alone is insufficient when reachability is UNREACHABLE.
Core runtime is known to be active.
Runtime activity is unknown, typically after IPC loss or stale observation.
TrayAgent process is confirmed running and able to present status.
Supervisor has scheduled or is performing a Core restart.
Observed Core state may be stale and cannot be used as current truth.
Validated independent observation proof: PID, start_nonce, source freshness, DeviceInstallation identity, and state-store identity all match.

intent_evaluation_rules: state_case determines lifecycle applicability, not full permission to execute
allowed true does not bypass client role validation
allowed true does not bypass operator authentication
allowed true does not bypass authorization context
zero or multiple matching state_cases means reject
UI success is shown only after Core acknowledgement

invalid_combinations.reason: Stopped, not-started and crashed Core do not have a confirmed reachable Core IPC endpoint.
Cached snapshot cannot provide CONFIRMED_CURRENT after IPC loss.

intent_evaluation_routes.description: Local DesktopShell/Tray lifecycle action; validates trigger/client role, uses intent-specific auth only, records local audit, and does not submit a Core command unless later contract explicitly adds one.
Versioned command to reachable Core; requires role, operator authentication, authorization context, Core IPC, Core revalidation, acknowledgement and audit.
Trusted OS event; no client role/operator auth/command auth required; Core notification/checkpoint is best-effort only if Core IPC is available; result is audited.
Bootstrapper stage after Core-issued MaintenanceAuthorization; cannot bypass prior operator/Core authorization.
